25:41-42.Internal Contradictions: Critics question why a supposedly universally evident prophet would be met with such profound mockery, arguing that this reflects human skepticism rather than a failure on the part of the divine message.

29:14.Biblical Contradictions: The Quran states that Noah preached for 950 years before the flood, whereas the biblical account in Genesis 9:29 records 950 years as his entire lifespan.

23:91.Biblical Contradictions: The Quran repeatedly denies that God has a Son, directly contradicting the central biblical doctrine of Jesus Christ as the Son of God.

10:68.Biblical Contradictions: By asserting that God has no son, this verse directly contradicts the core New Testament revelation of Jesus Christ as the divine Son of God.

112:1-4.Biblical Contradictions: Sura 112 explicitly denies the Christian doctrine of the Trinity and the eternal generation of the Son, contradicting the biblical understanding of God's nature.

37:6-8.Scientific Errors: The Quran describes stars as missiles thrown at eavesdropping demons, a cosmological view that conflicts with the modern scientific understanding of stars.

6:102.Biblical Contradictions: The strict unitarianism of this verse contradicts the biblical revelation of the Trinity and the deity of Christ.

17:101.Biblical Contradictions: The Quran mentions Moses bringing 'nine clear signs,' which conflicts with the biblical account in Exodus that records ten plagues upon Egypt.

7:133.Biblical Contradictions: The Quran includes a 'flood' among the plagues of Egypt, which contradicts the biblical record in Exodus that does not list a flood among the ten plagues.

20:85-87.Historical Errors & Anachronisms: The Quran claims a 'Samaritan' led the Israelites astray with the golden calf, which is a historical anachronism since the Samaritans did not exist as a people until centuries later.

19:27-28.Historical Errors & Anachronisms: The Quran addresses Mary, the mother of Jesus, as the 'sister of Aaron,' erroneously conflating her with the Old Testament figure Miriam, who lived over a millennium earlier.

3:35-36.Historical Errors & Anachronisms: This passage continues the historical conflation of Mary, the mother of Jesus, with the Old Testament Miriam, by associating her with Amram (Imran).

22:34.Biblical Contradictions: The Quran's assertion that God appointed a ritual of animal sacrifice for every community conflicts with the biblical teaching that such sacrifices were specific to the Mosaic covenant.

28:1-13.Biblical Contradictions: The Quran states that Pharaoh's wife adopted the infant Moses, directly contradicting the biblical account in Exodus which records that he was adopted by Pharaoh's daughter.

7:172-173.Biblical Contradictions: The Quranic concept of a pre-mortal covenant where all of Adam's descendants testified to God's lordship has no parallel or support in the biblical texts.

26:105.Biblical Contradictions: This verse inaccurately states that the people of Noah rejected multiple envoys, despite Noah being the sole prophet sent to them during the flood.

33:72.Scientific Errors: The Quran anthropomorphizes the heavens, earth, and mountains by claiming they were offered a moral trust but refused out of fear, which critics point out is a mythological description.

6:83-89.Biblical Contradictions: The Quran seems to suggest that all subsequent prophets were descendants of Abraham, contradicting the biblical account which identifies prophets from other lineages.

29:27.Biblical Contradictions: By claiming the prophetic office and the Book were placed exclusively in Abraham's descendants, the Quran contradicts the biblical presence of prophets outside the Abrahamic line.

10:94.Internal Contradictions: The Quran commands Muslims to verify their faith by asking Christians and Jews who read the prior scriptures, contradicting the common Islamic polemic that those same scriptures are hopelessly corrupted.

16:11.Scientific Errors: The Quran asserts that all fruits are created in pairs, a claim that is biologically inaccurate since many plants can reproduce without sexual pairing.

7:127.Biblical Contradictions: The Quran depicts Pharaoh ordering the slaughter of Israelite male children in response to Moses' preaching, contradicting the biblical account where the slaughter occurred before Moses was born.
